factors.summary: Overall demand is decelerating, with lower market demand for products and reduced sales volumes.

evidence.text: Due to changing market conditions, we are experiencing, and may continue to experience, lower market demand for our products. We have been experiencing, and may continue to experience, elevated commodity and other input costs, as well as employee-related cost inflation.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates a clear deceleration in demand with lower market demand and reduced sales volumes.

factors.summary: Order backlog or book-to-bill information is not explicitly provided in the text.

evidence.text: The text does not contain specific information about order backlog or book-to-bill trends.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No specific information is provided about order backlog or book-to-bill trends, making it impossible to determine a sentiment.

factors.summary: The company has successfully raised prices, which has partially offset unfavorable demand and cost pressures.

evidence.text: Higher net selling prices which increased sales by four percent and five percent for the three and six months ended June 30, 2023, respectively.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has successfully raised prices, partially offsetting unfavorable demand and cost pressures.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin products, partially offsetting unfavorable demand trends.

evidence.text: Higher net selling prices which increased sales by four percent and five percent for the three and six months ended June 30, 2023, respectively. These amounts were partially offset by: • Lower sales volume across all product categories decreased sales by 13 percent and 14 percent for the three and six months ended June 30, 2023, respectively.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shifting towards higher-margin products helps offset unfavorable demand trends.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating, with a 10% decrease in net sales for the three months ended June 30, 2023.

evidence.text: Net sales for the three months ended June 30, 2023 were $2.1 billion, which decreased 10 percent compared to the three months ended June 30, 2022.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is decelerating with a 10% decrease in net sales.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining, driven by lower sales volumes across all product categories.

evidence.text: Lower sales volume across all product categories decreased sales by 13 percent and 14 percent for the three and six months ended June 30, 2023, respectively.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Declining unit volumes due to lower sales across all product categories.

factors.summary: Both Plumbing Products and Decorative Architectural Products segments are experiencing decelerating demand, with lower sales volumes across all product categories.

evidence.text: Net sales in the Plumbing Products segment decreased 11 percent and 10 percent for the three and six months ended June 30, 2023, respectively. Net sales in the Decorative Architectural Products segment decreased eight percent and nine percent for the three and six months ended June 30, 2023, respectively.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Both segments are experiencing significant deceleration in demand with substantial decreases in sales volumes.

factors.summary: Input costs are elevated but showing some reduction, with the company aiming to offset them through productivity and pricing.

evidence.text: We have recently seen some reduction of certain costs, and we aim to offset the potential unfavorable impact of our costs and lower demand for our products with productivity improvement, pricing, and other initiatives. Increased commodity and other input costs for both periods. These amounts were partially offset by: • Higher net selling prices. • Cost savings initiatives. • Lower transportation costs.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While input costs are elevated, there are signs of reduction and efforts to offset them, leading to a mixed sentiment.

factors.summary: Labor costs are creating margin pressure due to employee-related cost inflation, though some cost savings initiatives are in place.

evidence.text: We have been experiencing, and may continue to experience, elevated commodity and other input costs, as well as employee-related cost inflation. These amounts were partially offset by: • Cost savings initiatives. • Lower transportation costs.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are rising, putting pressure on margins, though some savings initiatives are in place.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are improving due to higher net selling prices and cost savings initiatives, despite higher input costs and lower sales volumes.

evidence.text: Gross margin was positively impacted by higher net selling prices, cost savings initiatives, and lower transportation costs. Operating profit was positively impacted by higher net selling prices, cost savings initiatives, and lower transportation costs.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are improving due to higher net selling prices and cost savings.

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives are delivering measurable savings, contributing to improved margins despite higher input costs.

evidence.text: Cost savings initiatives. • Lower transportation costs. These amounts were partially offset by: • Lower sales volume. • Increased commodity and other input costs. • Unfavorable sales mix.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction initiatives are delivering measurable savings, contributing to improved margins.

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving due to productivity initiatives, contributing to margin expansion.

evidence.text: Productivity improvement, pricing, and other initiatives. Higher net selling prices. Cost savings initiatives. Lower transportation costs.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Operational efficiency is improving due to productivity initiatives, contributing to margin expansion.

factors.summary: Forward guidance is cautious, with management confident in long-term strategies but facing near-term challenges.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include lower demand, elevated input costs, and the need for continued cost management.

evidence.text: Due to changing market conditions, we are experiencing, and may continue to experience, lower market demand for our products. We have been experiencing, and may continue to experience, elevated commodity and other input costs, as well as employee-related cost inflation.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on brand strength, innovation, and disciplined capital allocation.

We remain confident in the fundamentals of our business and long-term strategy.

sentiment.label: positive

factors.summary: The balance sheet is strengthening with cash and cash investments of approximately $380 million at June 30, 2023, and the company remains in compliance with financial covenants.

evidence.text: Our current ratio was 1.7 to 1 and 1.6 to 1 at June 30, 2023 and December 31, 2022, respectively. We believe that our present cash balance and cash flows from operations, and borrowing availability under our revolving credit agreement, are sufficient to fund our near-term working capital and other investment needs.

sentiment.label: positive

factors.summary: Management's confidence is evident through their ongoing share repurchase activities and their affirmation of the company's long-term strategy, despite facing near-term challenges.

evidence.text: The following table provides information regarding the repurchase of our common stock for the three months ended June 30, 2023 under the 2022 share repurchase authorization:

We remain confident in the fundamentals of our business and long-term strategy. We believe that our strong financial position and cash flow generation, together with our investments in our industry-leading branded building products, our continued focus on innovation and disciplined capital allocation, will allow us to drive long-term growth and create value for our shareholders.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management shows confidence through share repurchases and affirming long-term strategy.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are prioritized with a $2 billion share repurchase authorization, indicating management confidence.

evidence.text: The following table provides information regarding the repurchase of our common stock for the three months ended June 30, 2023 under the 2022 share repurchase authorization:

We repurchased and retired approximately 1.6 million shares of our common stock in the six months ended June 30, 2023 for approximately $81 million. This included 0.2 million shares to offset the dilutive impact of restricted stock units granted in the six months ended June 30, 2023. At June 30, 2023, we had approximately $1.9 billion remaining under the 2022 authorization. Consistent with our past practice and as part of our long-term capital allocation strategy, we anticipate using approximately $350 million of cash for share repurchases (including shares which will be purchased to offset any dilution from restricted stock units granted as part of our compensation program) in 2023.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Significant share repurchase indicates prioritization of shareholder returns.
